name: non-degenerate
statement_text:
A starting method S defined by the generalized Runge–
                                                                   (i)
Kutta methods (530a), for i = 1, 2, . . . , r, is ‘degenerate’ if b0 = 0, for
i = 1, 2, . . . , r, and ‘non-degenerate’ otherwise.

variables.name: S
variables.definition: a starting method, defined as a sequence of r generalized Runge–Kutta methods S1, S2, ..., Sr

variables.name: r
variables.definition: the number of generalized Runge–Kutta methods in the starting method S

variables.name: S_i
variables.definition: the i-th generalized Runge–Kutta method in the starting method S, for i = 1, 2, ..., r

variables.name: b0^{(i)}
variables.definition: a coefficient (presumably from the generalized Runge–Kutta method definition) for the i-th method S_i

equations.content: the definition of the generalized Runge–Kutta methods that constitute the starting method S (exact form not provided in the preceding text, but referenced as the methods defining S)

preamble: A starting method S is a sequence of r generalized Runge–Kutta methods used to initialize a general linear method. Degeneracy depends on whether all these methods have a zero coefficient b0^{(i)}.
